examples.title: FTP Client Connection Process
examples.description: Pseudocode showing basic FTP connection and file download steps
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the FTP client process: connecting to control port, authenticating with USER/PASS, setting transfer mode, entering passive mode, creating data connection, downloading file via RETR command, and properly closing connections.

examples.title: FTP Server Process
examples.description: Pseudocode for FTP server handling client requests
examples.code:
// FTP Server Main Process
BEGIN
 // Create control socket on port 21
 controlSocket = createServerSocket(21)
 
 WHILE serverRunning DO
 // Wait for client connection
 clientSocket = acceptConnection(controlSocket)
 
 // Start new thread for client
 START new thread handleClient(clientSocket)
 ENDWHILE
END

PROCEDURE handleClient(clientSocket)
 // Send welcome message
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "220 FTP Server Ready\n")
 
 authenticated = FALSE
 
 WHILE clientConnected DO
 // Read command from client
 command = readCommand(clientSocket)
 
 IF command = "USER" THEN
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "331 Username OK, need password\n")
 
 ELSE IF command = "PASS" THEN
 IF authenticateUser(username, password) THEN
 authenticated = TRUE
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "230 User logged in\n")
 ELSE
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "530 Not logged in\n")
 ENDIF
 
 ELSE IF command = "LIST" THEN
 IF authenticated THEN
 dataSocket = createDataConnection()
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "150 File status okay\n")
 sendDirectoryListing(dataSocket)
 close(dataSocket)
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "226 Transfer complete\n")
 ELSE
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "530 Not logged in\n")
 ENDIF
 
 ELSE IF command = "RETR" THEN
 IF authenticated THEN
 dataSocket = createDataConnection()
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "150 Opening data connection\n")
 sendFile(dataSocket, filename)
 close(dataSocket)
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "226 Transfer complete\n")
 ELSE
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "530 Not logged in\n")
 ENDIF
 
 ELSE IF command = "QUIT" THEN
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "221 Goodbye\n")
 close(clientSocket)
 EXIT
 
 ELSE
 sendResponse(clientSocket, "502 Command not implemented\n")
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates FTP server architecture: listening on port 21, accepting client connections, creating separate threads for each client, handling authentication, processing commands (USER, PASS, LIST, RETR, QUIT), and managing data connections for file transfers.

examples.title: Active vs Passive FTP Mode Comparison
examples.description: Comparison pseudocode showing connection establishment in both modes
examples.explanation: This shows the key difference: In Active mode, client binds to a port and waits for server to connect. In Passive mode, server binds to a port and client connects to it. This difference is crucial for firewall/NAT traversal.
